free_text: New data on activation cross section for deuteron
induced reactions on ytterbium up to 50 MeV
Natural composition Yb foils with 22.88
micron thickness
For measurements,
the well-known stacked foil irradiation technique and
high resolution gamma-spectrometry were used. Yb
metal foils, interleaved with Al foils for monitoring
beam characteristics were stacked and irradiated at
the UCL (LLN) cyclotron. The Yb foils were irradiated
together with Nd foils. The irradiated stack had a
complex structure, consisting of 19-times repeated
sequence of Nd (100 micron), Yb (22.88 um) and
Al(49.06 micron), interleaved with Al(156.6 micron,
103.43 micron, 49.06 micron) energy degraders. The
activity of each samples was assessed in 3
measurement series. Decay data were taken from NUDAT
includes the uncertainty of primary beam
energy, target thickness, energy straggling and
correction to monitor reaction
The uncertainty of the cross section is
calculated as the quadratic sums of the following
errors:
beam current (7%)
beamloss corrections (max 1.5%)
target thickness (1%)
detector efficiency (5%)
peak area determination and counting
statistics (1.0-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL-2013
ALICE-IPPE-D, EMPIRE-II-D.
The measured experimental data were compared
to those found in the literature.
